name: Comprehensive Guidelines for the Supervision of Major Banks, etc.
description: FSA-labeled "preliminary version" English translation, first published June 8, 2022, since amended. A 513-page, ~22,700-line bank-supervision manual; cybersecurity content is concentrated in section III-3-7 ("Information Technology (IT) System Risk", covering general IT risk, ATM system security, and interbank system/network use) and III-3-8 (Internet Banking).
relationToCloudSecurity: See registry/control/fsa.go.jp.json in the SecID repo (CloudSecurityAlliance/SecID) for cloud/AI security relevance notes on this source.
relationToAISecurity: See the paired SecID registry entry.

license.notes: Issued directly by a national government ministry/agency as an official notification/circular -- plausibly excluded from copyright under Japan's Copyright Act Article 13(2) ("notifications, instructions, circulars, and other documents similar thereto issued by national or local government organs"), but this is a less clear-cut case than primary legislation (Art. 13(1)) and has not been legally verified. Treat as moderate-confidence permitted, not certain. Official FSA-published translation, though explicitly labeled preliminary/non-authoritative by the FSA itself.

current_extraction.notes: Full 513-page manual downloaded and extracted (not encrypted, plain pdftotext -layout worked). Given its size and that only a small fraction concerns cybersecurity, only the III-3-7 excerpt (lines 12689-13301 of the full extraction, ~613 lines) is committed here rather than the whole manual -- the full raw PDF is kept locally (gitignored) if the rest is needed later.

scope.notes: Excerpt only (cybersecurity-relevant section). Full manual acquired locally but not committed in full.
